title: verbascum agrimoniifolium subsp. syriacum
intro: Verbascum agrimoniifolium subsp. syriacum, also known as Syrian mullein, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia Minor. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which make it useful for treating a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Syrian mullein include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Syrian mullein is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to skin conditions or used as a mouthwash to treat oral infections.
